page: OVHcloud a100-720 is a KVM-hypervised GPU-accelerated server built on the x86_64 CPU architecture. It features 60 dedicated vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz, paired with 720.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 12.0 GB per core. The primary hardware accelerator configuration consists of four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs, providing a combined total of 320 GB of VRAM.

Local storage is provided by a 500 GB NVMe SSD, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps. One complimentary public IPv4 address is included. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high density of GPU and memory resources relative to the dedicated CPU core count, making it a specialized option for workloads requiring massive parallel processing capabilities rather than high local storage capacity.

This hardware profile is designed for workloads such as large-scale deep learning model training, high-performance computing simulations, large-scale data science jobs, and complex parallel processing tasks that leverage multi-GPU configurations and substantial system memory.
description: OVHcloud a100-720 is a KVM-hypervised GPU-accelerated server designed for resource-intensive parallel computing workloads. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 60 dedicated vCPUs running at 3.0 GHz and 720.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.0 GB of RAM per core. The server is equipped with four NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs, delivering a total of 320 GB of VRAM. Storage is limited to a single 500 GB NVMe SSD, while networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps. One public IPv4 address is included. This configuration represents a high-density resource profile optimized for parallel processing rather than local storage capacity. It is suited for workloads such as deep learning model training, high-performance computing, and large-scale data science tasks.
